mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger negative equity repricing over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.3pp threshold, invalidating the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output that reduces corporate profit margins and forces negative S&P 500 repricing over one month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO declines remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth accelerating above 3.0% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in > 70% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.0% threshold, refuting the tightening fear mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate indicates labor market inefficiency and hidden weakness that drives negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE levels shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue expansion and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth, or the signal loses predictive significance (p > 0.05) when tested across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing federal funds rate hikes signals resilient supply-side capacity that absorbs monetary tightening without earnings compression, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns fail to materialize when INDPRO growth exceeds FEDFUNDS hikes, with the interaction effect showing negative or null predictive accuracy in walk-forward tests.
